VOL LANDSCAPE & ANALYTICS

The VIX at 27.5 sits in backwardation with VIX3M trading 190bp below, marking the steepest inversion in six weeks as dealers prepare for vol reset. Broad equity IV ranks have spiked to 90th percentile across SPY, EEM, and IWM while put skew reaches extreme 99th percentile levels. Credit spreads remain contained despite equity vol expansion, creating a cross-asset divergence that typically resolves within 48 hours.

VRP: Front-month IV trading 4-6 points above realized vol across major indices — aggressive vol selling premium available for patient capital
SKEW: Put skew at 99th percentile in SPY/QQQ signals maximum defensive positioning and creates asymmetric call upside reward
TERM STRUCTURE: Steep backwardation in high-beta names (AMD, META, ARM) with front-week premiums 15-20% rich to fair value

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:31 ET
META explodes with 300K call blocks despite bearish technicals while ARM two-way flow surges on CPU entry
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. META sees massive call accumulation (300K Jan contracts) against death cross technicals — unusual conviction play into earnings season.
  • 2. Index-single name P/C divergence (1.43 vs 0.87) signals institutional dispersion hedging ahead of Core PCE tomorrow.
  • 3. ARM's explosive two-way flow (calls +122%, puts +205%) reflects market uncertainty around CPU market entry announcement timing.
NARRATIVE — vol expansion pre-PCE

META's options activity defies its deteriorating technical backdrop, with three massive January call blocks totaling 300,000 contracts across 700C, 750C, and 800C strikes despite the stock showing death cross formation and RSI at 36. The flow concentration at elevated strikes suggests positioning for an earnings catalyst rather than technical bounce, especially given Point72's recent $406M reduction and Druckenmiller's complete exit from the name. The call-heavy positioning (P/C 0.40) conflicts with the weak chart setup, creating a high-conviction contrarian signal. The P/C divergence between index puts (1.43) and single-name calls (0.87) reveals the classic institutional dispersion hedge into tomorrow's Core PCE print, with negative gamma at $-17.1B on SPY amplifying any volatility expansion. This structure typically precedes macro event volatility, where managers hedge index downside while maintaining single-name upside exposure. ARM exemplifies this tension with explosive two-way flow following its CPU market entry announcement — calls +122% and puts +205% as the market prices binary outcomes around competitive positioning against Intel and AMD. The Vol High/Skew Flat divergence across six names (IBIT, EEM, XBI, KWEB, XLC, XLU) combined with 11 names in backwardation suggests elevated near-term event risk pricing, consistent with tomorrow's PCE catalyst and Friday's NFP creating compressed vol term structures ahead of Fed policy implications.

Layer 1: Sentiment -4Layer 2: GEX -$17.1BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.43/0.87Layer 4: 11 names backwardationLayer 9: Core PCE 1d

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
